title: Database Backup and Recovery Quiz
description: Test your fundamental knowledge about database backup and recovery in modern web applications.

questions.question: What is the primary role of 'Database Backup and Recovery' in a web application?
options: To store more data on a single machine.
To provide a safety net for accidental deletion, hardware failure, and malicious attacks like ransomware.
To automatically generate a backup of the database.
To make the application more secure.

questions.question: What's the '3-2-1 Rule' of backups in modern computing?
options: A way to encrypt files using a secret key.
Keep 3 copies of your data, on 2 different media, with at least 1 off-site copy.
A type of database indexing.
A way to automatically compress files.

questions.question: What's the primary difference between a 'Full' backup and an 'Incremental' backup?
options: Full backup is faster.
Full is a complete copy of all data; Incremental only contains data that has changed since the most recent backup.
Full is the only way to restore correctly.
None of the above.

questions.question: What is 'Logical Backup' (Dump) and why is it useful?
options: A way to encrypt data before deleting it.
Exporting the database structure and data into a SQL file (e.g., using pg_dump) which is portable and easy to read.
A type of database indexing.
A way to automatically compress files.

questions.question: How can you truly verify that your database backup strategy is effective?
options: By daily checking the size of your backup files.
By performing periodic 'Recovery Drills' where you restore your production data into a separate staging environment to verify it works.
By asking the database engine to check for corruption.
None of the above.
